evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Endpoints used (api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast, archive-api.open-meteo.com/v1/archive, geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search) and params (latitude, longitude, hourly=temperature_2m, timezone, forecast_days, start_date/end_date) are internally consistent and match known Open-Meteo surface; no version drift detected. docs_unavailable=true, internally consistent across endpoints.
evidence.executability: Bash and Python/JSX blocks are syntactically valid. Minor issue: pip install of 'cors-middleware==1.0.1' is unnecessary (CORS is built into FastAPI/Starlette) and may fail to install or be irrelevant; rest works. No sandbox-infeasible patterns.
evidence.completeness: (a) No API key needed — explicitly stated; (b) bash install commands present; (c) env template present with exact var names matching code usage; (d) FastAPI main.py present; (e) React App/components present; (f) test command (pytest + curl) present. All six pieces extractable.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Cites open-meteo.com/en/docs and /docs/historical-weather-api (official Open-Meteo docs domain), placed in numbered sources at end and inline-referenced via [3][11]. No fabricated URLs detected.

rubric_notes: Long-tail handling applied (docs_unavailable). Spurious 'cors-middleware' pip dep is a minor executability nit. Otherwise solid, internally consistent integration.
